name: agent-friendly-services
description: A community-maintained, evidence-backed directory of service entry points for AI agents.

spec.entrypoint_semantics: A missing entrypoint means "no known URL", not "confirmed absent".

categories.name: AI Models
categories.description: Model and inference APIs (LLMs, embeddings, media generation).

categories.name: Agent Tooling
categories.description: Agent infrastructure, MCP tooling, orchestration services.

categories.name: Code Execution
categories.description: Code sandboxes, ephemeral compute, browser automation.

categories.name: Developer Tools
categories.description: Code hosting, CI, testing, API tooling, error tracking.

categories.name: Cloud / Hosting
categories.description: Clouds, deployment platforms, edge and serverless hosting.

categories.name: Web Search / Data
categories.description: Search APIs, crawling and scraping, data providers.

docs.name: Documentation
docs.description: Main documentation site. Must be readable without login.

openapi.name: OpenAPI
openapi.description: OpenAPI/machine-readable interface definition (spec file or official repo).

llms_txt.name: llms.txt
llms_txt.description: Official llms.txt index for the docs site.

pricing.name: Pricing
pricing.description: Public pricing page or billing model description.

api_keys.name: API Keys
api_keys.description: Documentation for creating and managing API keys/tokens.

cli.name: CLI
cli.description: Official command-line tool that can perform core operations.

sdks.name: SDKs
sdks.description: Index of officially maintained SDKs/client libraries.

mcp_official.name: Official MCP
mcp_official.description: Official MCP server or officially supported MCP integration.

mcp_community.name: Community MCP
mcp_community.description: Trusted community MCP servers (list). Informational only; never feeds a badge.

agent_docs.name: Agent Docs
agent_docs.description: Documentation written specifically for agents/LLM tooling.

errors.name: Errors
errors.description: Error codes and error structure documentation.

self_serve_signup.name: Self-serve signup
self_serve_signup.description: An account can be created without sales involvement.

api_key_self_serve.name: Self-serve API keys
api_key_self_serve.description: Users can create API keys themselves.

oauth_support.name: OAuth
oauth_support.description: Supports OAuth or an equivalent standard authorization flow.

scoped_tokens.name: Scoped tokens
scoped_tokens.description: Tokens can be restricted in permission scope (incl. service accounts / machine identities — note specifics).

sandbox_or_test_mode.name: Sandbox / test mode
sandbox_or_test_mode.description: A test environment, test keys, or a sandbox exists.

free_tier_or_trial.name: Free tier / trial
free_tier_or_trial.description: Can be tried at low or no cost.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.name: Self-serve upgrade
self_serve_paid_upgrade.description: Paying, upgrading, or topping up requires no sales contact.

usage_dashboard.name: Usage dashboard
usage_dashboard.description: Users can inspect their API/product usage.

idempotency.name: Idempotency
idempotency.description: Supports idempotency keys or an equivalent safe-retry mechanism.

versioning_policy.name: Versioning policy
versioning_policy.description: API versioning or a clear breaking-change/deprecation policy exists.

automation_permitted.name: Automation permitted
automation_permitted.description: Terms of service allow automated/agent usage (evidence = ToS/bot policy clause).

providers.name: Airtable
providers.summary: Spreadsheet-database hybrid with a REST API, scoped personal access tokens, OAuth, webhooks, and documented rate limits.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Personal access tokens are created self-serve in the builder hub.

scoped_tokens.notes: Tokens carry explicit scopes and are restricted to selected bases/workspaces.

token_revocation.notes: Revocation is documented in the personal-access-token guide.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Free bases serve as test environments; no separate sandbox documented.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: Team plan upgrade is self-serve; Enterprise requires sales.

versioning_policy.notes: An API changelog exists; no formal versioning/deprecation policy page recorded yet.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Anthropic
providers.summary: Claude model APIs with agent-focused documentation, llms.txt, and the company behind the MCP standard itself.

oauth_support.notes: No public OAuth flow for the API; API keys are the standard auth.

scoped_tokens.notes: Keys are scoped per workspace (limits and separation per workspace); no per-permission scoping.

token_revocation.notes: Keys can be disabled in the console; needs a canonical docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated test mode documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: API is prepaid usage-based; no standing free tier documented.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: Prepaid credits and usage tiers are self-serve in the console.

usage_dashboard.notes: Console usage page; rate-limit headers expose remaining quota programmatically.

versioning_policy.notes: anthropic-version header with documented version behavior.

automation_permitted.notes: Usage policies not yet reviewed for autonomous agent account usage.

notes: Anthropic authored the MCP standard; no first-party MCP server exposing the Anthropic API was found at review time (mcp_official intentionally absent).
Claude Code is listed as cli — it is an agent CLI rather than an API-management CLI.

providers.name: Apify
providers.summary: Web scraping and automation platform with thousands of ready-made actors, a versioned API, llms.txt, and an official MCP server.

scoped_tokens.notes: Account-level API tokens; per-permission scoping not documented.

token_revocation.notes: Tokens manageable in console settings; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The free plan serves as a test environment; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan with monthly platform credits.

usage_dashboard.notes: Console shows usage and billing; needs a docs link as evidence.

versioning_policy.notes: Versioned API (v2).

automation_permitted.notes: The platform exists for automation; ToS/target-site obligations not yet reviewed and recorded.

notes: mcp.apify.com hosts the official remote MCP server; the docs page above explains setup.

providers.name: Atlassian (Jira & Confluence)
providers.summary: Jira, Confluence and the Atlassian Cloud platform — REST APIs, an official remote MCP server (OAuth 2.1), and the acli CLI.
providers.scope: Atlassian Cloud (Jira, Confluence, JSM, Bitbucket)

api_key_self_serve.notes: Personal API tokens created self-serve; the MCP server uses OAuth 2.1 or API tokens.

oauth_support.notes: OAuth 2.0 (3LO) apps; the official remote MCP server authenticates via OAuth 2.1.

scoped_tokens.notes: Granular OAuth scopes; API tokens can also be scoped.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Product sandboxes are a Premium/Enterprise feature; free sites serve as informal test instances.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan for up to 10 users.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Brave Search API
providers.summary: Independent web search index with a developer API, self-serve registration, and a free plan.
providers.scope: Brave Search API (Data for Search / Data for AI plans); the consumer search engine is not assessed.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Keys are issued in the API dashboard after registration.

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The free plan serves as a low-risk trial; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan with monthly query quota.

usage_dashboard.notes: The API dashboard shows usage; needs a public docs link as evidence.

idempotency.notes: Read-only search API; requests are inherently repeatable.

automation_permitted.notes: API terms not yet reviewed and recorded.

notes: Detailed API docs live inside the dashboard domain but are publicly readable without login (verified at review time).

providers.name: Browserbase
providers.summary: Headless browser infrastructure for AI agents and automation, with session APIs and an official MCP server.

oauth_support.notes: API-key auth only, as far as documented.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The product itself is an ephemeral, isolated browser session — every run is a sandbox.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan with limited browser hours.

usage_dashboard.notes: Dashboard shows session usage; needs a docs link as evidence.

automation_permitted.notes: Running automated browsers is the product's stated purpose; your obligations toward target sites remain your own.

notes: Stagehand (the company's agent framework) is a separate open-source project and not assessed here.

providers.name: Cartesia
providers.summary: Low-latency voice models (Sonic TTS, Ink STT) with a documented API, official MCP server, llms.txt, and a free tier.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Playground exists for interactive testing.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Cerebras Inference
providers.summary: Wafer-scale inference for open models at very high tokens/sec, OpenAI-compatible API, llms.txt, and a standing free tier.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free tier with daily token quota.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Chroma
providers.summary: Open-source embedding database with a hosted Chroma Cloud, official CLI, official MCP server, and llms.txt.
providers.scope: Chroma Cloud + open-source

api_key_self_serve.notes: Cloud getting-started flow issues API keys self-serve; CLI login also supported.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Open-source Chroma runs locally/in-memory, serving as a test environment.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Chroma Cloud includes starter credits; open-source is free.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Cloudflare
providers.summary: Edge network, Workers serverless platform, storage, and AI services with agent-focused docs and official MCP servers.
providers.scope: Developer platform (Workers, R2, D1, AI) and core API. Enterprise network products not assessed.

oauth_support.notes: No public OAuth flow for third-party apps found; API tokens are the standard auth.

scoped_tokens.notes: API tokens support granular per-resource, per-permission scoping.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No global sandbox; wrangler local dev and the Workers free plan serve as a test environment.

usage_dashboard.notes: Billing docs cover dashboard-based plan, subscription and usage management.

versioning_policy.notes: Stable v4 API plus a published deprecation process.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed for automated/agent account usage.

providers.name: Cohere
providers.summary: Enterprise LLM platform (command, embed, rerank) with llms.txt, documented API versioning, free trial keys, and error/rate-limit docs.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Trial and production keys are created self-serve in the dashboard (the rate-limit docs define both key types).

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in the dashboard; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Free trial keys are explicitly positioned for evaluation/testing, rate-limited separately from production keys; no isolated sandbox environment.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Trial keys are free.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: Production keys with pay-as-you-go billing are self-serve.

versioning_policy.notes: Date-based API versions documented.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Composio
providers.summary: Tool and integration layer for AI agents (hundreds of app connectors with managed auth), with llms.txt and a hosted MCP directory.

api_key_self_serve.notes: API keys are created in the dashboard per the quickstart; needs the canonical docs link as evidence.

oauth_support.notes: Composio manages OAuth to third-party apps on the agent's behalf; whether its own API offers OAuth is unverified.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free tier for developers.

automation_permitted.notes: The product exists to let agents act on integrated apps.

providers.name: Datadog
providers.summary: Observability platform with a full REST API, llms.txt, documented OAuth for integrations, rate limits, and webhooks.

api_key_self_serve.notes: API keys and application keys are created self-serve in organization settings.

oauth_support.notes: OAuth2 for integrations and apps.

scoped_tokens.notes: Application keys support scoping in newer accounts; the api-app-keys page did not confirm on probe — needs the canonical docs link as evidence.

token_revocation.notes: Keys are manageable in organization settings; the api-app-keys page did not confirm revocation wording on probe — needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Trial orgs serve as test environments; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free tier (limited hosts) and 14-day trial.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: Pro/Enterprise per-host plans are self-serve; volume discounts go through sales.

versioning_policy.notes: Endpoints are path-versioned (v1/v2) in the reference; no formal deprecation-window policy page recorded.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Deepgram
providers.summary: Speech-to-text and voice AI API with a public OpenAPI spec, llms.txt, scoped API keys, and $200 free credit without a card.

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

scoped_tokens.notes: Keys carry roles/scopes and optional expiration dates.

token_revocation.notes: Keys deletable in the console; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The free credit serves as a de-facto trial environment; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: $200 free credit at signup, no card required.

versioning_policy.notes: Endpoints are path-versioned (v1); no formal deprecation policy page recorded yet.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: DeepSeek
providers.summary: OpenAI-compatible LLM API (DeepSeek-V3/R1) with transparent per-token pricing, a detailed changelog, and self-serve keys.

self_serve_signup.notes: Platform sign-up is self-serve; the console blocks automated probes (HTTP 405), so expect inconclusive link checks.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Keys are created in the platform console; the page blocks automated probes (HTTP 405).

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in the console; needs a docs link as evidence.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Pricing is pay-per-token top-up; no standing free tier documented.

versioning_policy.notes: Model versions are dated and announced in the changelog; no formal deprecation policy page recorded.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Discord
providers.summary: Chat platform with a versioned bot/OAuth2 API, official OpenAPI spec (preview), webhooks, and documented rate limits.
providers.scope: The Discord developer platform (bots, OAuth2 apps, webhooks); the consumer client is not assessed.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Bot tokens are issued instantly when you create an application in the developer portal.

scoped_tokens.notes: OAuth2 scopes plus per-guild bot permission flags.

token_revocation.notes: Token revocation endpoint documented; bot tokens can be reset in the portal.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No formal sandbox; standard practice is a private test server. Needs official guidance as evidence.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: The API is free to use.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: No paid API tier; monetization products are user-facing.

versioning_policy.notes: Explicit API versioning (v10) with documented deprecation of old versions.

automation_permitted.notes: Automation through the bot API is the supported path; automating user accounts ("self-bots") is prohibited by the developer terms (ToS page blocks probes, so the docs portal is cited).

notes: The official OpenAPI spec is published by Discord but marked public preview / subject to change.

providers.name: Dropbox
providers.summary: File storage and sync with a scoped-OAuth HTTP API, self-serve app creation, and webhooks.

api_key_self_serve.notes: App creation in the developer console is self-serve; access tokens can be generated there for development.

scoped_tokens.notes: Scoped access is the standard model; apps request granular scopes.

token_revocation.notes: An /auth/token/revoke endpoint exists in the HTTP reference (client-rendered, not probe-verifiable); needs a stable docs anchor as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Development apps with limited users serve as a de-facto test mode; needs the canonical docs framing as evidence.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free Basic plan; the API works on it.

versioning_policy.notes: API v2 is stable and additive; no formal deprecation policy page recorded.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: E2B
providers.summary: Isolated cloud sandboxes for running AI-generated code, with llms.txt, an official MCP server, and self-serve keys.

self_serve_signup.notes: Sign-up is self-serve from the homepage; the dashboard URL redirect-loops for unauthenticated probes.

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in the dashboard; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The product itself is an isolated, ephemeral sandbox per session.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free hobby tier.

automation_permitted.notes: Running agent-generated code is the product's stated purpose.

providers.name: ElevenLabs
providers.summary: Voice AI (TTS, STT, agents) with a public OpenAPI spec, llms.txt, an official MCP server, and a free tier.

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

scoped_tokens.notes: The key settings UI offers permission restrictions; needs a docs link as evidence.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in settings; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: The free tier serves as a de-facto test environment; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan with monthly character quota.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Exa
providers.summary: Search API built for AI — semantic web search, content retrieval, and research endpoints with an official MCP server.

oauth_support.notes: API-key auth only, as far as documented.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in dashboard; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Free credits serve as a low-risk trial; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free starter credits.

usage_dashboard.notes: Dashboard shows usage; needs a docs link as evidence.

idempotency.notes: Read-only search/retrieval API; requests are inherently repeatable.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed.

providers.name: fal.ai
providers.summary: Generative media platform (image, video, audio models) with queue/streaming APIs, an official CLI/serving framework, llms.txt, and self-serve keys.

self_serve_signup.notes: GitHub-based self-serve signup; dashboard reachable immediately after.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free credits for new accounts; pay-per-use afterwards.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Firecrawl
providers.summary: Web scraping and crawling API that turns websites into LLM-ready markdown, with an official MCP server.

oauth_support.notes: API-key auth only, as far as documented.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Free credits serve as a low-risk trial; no separate sandbox documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free plan with monthly credits.

idempotency.notes: Scrape/crawl jobs are re-runnable reads; no state mutation to protect.

automation_permitted.notes: The product exists for automation, but ToS/robots handling not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Fireworks AI
providers.summary: Fast open-model inference and fine-tuning with an OpenAI-compatible API, official firectl CLI, llms.txt, and published pricing.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free credits for new developers.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Fly.io
providers.summary: Run full-stack apps and machines close to users, with a spec'd Machines API, scoped macaroon tokens, and official MCP docs.

scoped_tokens.notes: Macaroon-based tokens can be narrowly scoped (org, app, deploy-only).

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated sandbox; cheap ephemeral machines are commonly used for testing.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Pay-as-you-go only for new customers; legacy Hobby/Launch/Scale free allowances are honored only for organizations that had them before sunset.

usage_dashboard.notes: Per-org billing dashboard with invoices and per-second usage.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed; notably, fly.io/llms.txt carries an explicit AI-agent access policy asking automated LLM clients to identify themselves via an AI-Agent request header (telemetry only, not auth).

notes: fly.io/llms.txt publishes an explicit AI-agent access policy: automated LLM clients are asked to identify via an AI-Agent request header (telemetry only, not authentication).

providers.name: Gemini API
providers.summary: Google's Gemini model APIs via AI Studio, with generous free tier and documented API versioning.
providers.scope: Gemini API via Google AI Studio (ai.google.dev); Vertex AI is not assessed.

oauth_support.notes: API keys via AI Studio are the standard auth; OAuth exists in the broader Google Cloud surface, out of scope here.

scoped_tokens.notes: Keys are project-bound in AI Studio; no per-permission scoping documented on the Gemini API surface.

token_revocation.notes: Keys can be deleted in AI Studio; needs a canonical docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated test mode; the free tier serves as a low-risk environment.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Documented free tier with rate limits.

versioning_policy.notes: v1/v1beta versions are documented.

automation_permitted.notes: Gemini API additional terms not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: GitHub
providers.summary: Code hosting, collaboration, and automation with REST and GraphQL APIs, an official CLI, and an official MCP server.
providers.scope: Core platform (repos, issues, PRs, REST/GraphQL APIs). Actions/Packages/Copilot not assessed separately.

scoped_tokens.notes: Fine-grained personal access tokens with per-repo, per-permission scoping; GitHub Apps for machine identities.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated API sandbox; free accounts and throwaway orgs are commonly used for testing.

usage_dashboard.notes: Billing/usage pages plus a rate-limit API endpoint for API quota introspection.

idempotency.notes: No documented idempotency-key mechanism for the REST API.

versioning_policy.notes: Date-based API versioning with a documented deprecation process.

automation_permitted.notes: API automation and bots are permitted within rate limits and acceptable-use rules; scraping restrictions apply.

notes: Multi-product platform; this entry covers the core developer platform only (see scope).

providers.name: GitLab
providers.summary: DevOps platform with REST and GraphQL APIs, scoped tokens, llms.txt, and an official CLI.
providers.scope: gitlab.com SaaS + REST/GraphQL APIs; self-managed GitLab is not assessed.

self_serve_signup.notes: Free-tier signup is self-serve; the signup page itself blocks HEAD probes, so the pricing page serves as evidence.

scoped_tokens.notes: Personal, project, and group access tokens with documented scopes.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated API sandbox; free accounts and throwaway groups are commonly used for testing.

versioning_policy.notes: Stable v4 REST API with a documented deprecation and removals process.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Grafana (Grafana Cloud)
providers.summary: Observability platform (dashboards, metrics, logs, traces) with a documented HTTP API, official MCP server, llms.txt, and a standing free cloud tier.
providers.scope: Grafana Cloud + open-source

api_key_self_serve.notes: Service-account tokens replace legacy API keys; created self-serve.

scoped_tokens.notes: Service accounts carry role-based (and RBAC) permissions.

token_revocation.notes: Tokens can be revoked/expired from the service-accounts UI.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Open-source Grafana runs locally as a full test environment.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Standing free Grafana Cloud tier.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Groq
providers.summary: Ultra-low-latency LLM inference with an OpenAI-compatible API, llms.txt, and self-serve keys with a free tier.

oauth_support.notes: API keys are the standard auth.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in console; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated test mode; the free tier serves as a low-risk environment.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free tier with rate limits.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Hugging Face
providers.summary: Model hub and inference platform with fine-grained tokens, OAuth, an official MCP server, and a full Hub API.
providers.scope: Hub + Hub API + Inference Providers; self-hosted libraries are not assessed.

oauth_support.notes: "Sign in with Hugging Face" OAuth/OpenID Connect for third-party apps.

scoped_tokens.notes: Fine-grained tokens with per-repo and per-permission scoping.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: No dedicated sandbox; free accounts serve as a test environment.

usage_dashboard.notes: Billing/usage pages exist in settings; needs a public docs link as evidence.

automation_permitted.notes: Hub ToS not yet reviewed for automated/agent usage; API automation is clearly normal practice.

providers.name: Jina AI
providers.summary: Search-foundation APIs (Reader for URL-to-markdown, embeddings, reranker, deep search) with an official remote MCP server, an agent-targeted llms.txt, and a keyless trial path.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Dashboard issues keys; a starter token quota works without registration for some endpoints (r.jina.ai).

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Rate-limited keyless access to Reader serves as a de facto trial; needs the canonical docs link as evidence.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free token allowance for new keys.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Lark
providers.summary: Collaboration suite (messaging, docs, calendar) with an open platform, llms.txt, an official CLI with 200+ commands and agent skills, and an official OpenAPI MCP server.
providers.scope: Lark Open Platform (international larksuite.com edition); the Feishu (China) edition has a separate developer portal.

self_serve_signup.notes: Team sign-up is self-serve from the homepage; developer console access follows from any account.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Creating a custom app in the developer console yields app_id/app_secret self-serve; tokens are exchanged via API.

oauth_support.notes: OAuth authorization-code flow for user_access_token.

scoped_tokens.notes: Apps request granular API scopes that admins approve; tokens carry only granted scopes.

token_revocation.notes: App secrets can be reset in the developer console; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Developer documentation mentions test companies/sandbox tenants; needs the canonical docs link as evidence.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Free Starter plan; open platform APIs available on it.

self_serve_paid_upgrade.notes: Pro plan pricing is published; whether upgrade completes without sales contact is unverified.

versioning_policy.notes: Endpoints are path-versioned (v1/v2...); no formal deprecation policy page recorded yet.

automation_permitted.notes: The open platform exists to build bots and integrations against workspace data.

providers.name: Lemon Squeezy
providers.summary: Merchant-of-record payments for digital products/SaaS with a JSON:API REST API, documented test mode, and self-serve keys.

self_serve_signup.notes: Store activation (to take live payments) involves a review step; test mode works immediately.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Documented test mode with test cards, separate test-mode API keys.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: Fee-per-transaction model; no subscription to trial.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Linear
providers.summary: Issue tracking and product planning with a GraphQL API, llms.txt, an official MCP server, and webhooks.

api_key_self_serve.notes: Personal API keys are created in settings, documented in the GraphQL guide.

oauth_support.notes: OAuth 2.0 for applications is documented in the developer docs.

token_revocation.notes: Keys manageable in settings; needs a docs link as evidence.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Free workspaces serve as test environments; no separate sandbox documented.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Luma AI (Dream Machine)
providers.summary: Dream Machine video and image generation via the Luma API, with llms.txt and published API pricing.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: API is pay-per-use; consumer app has free generations, API trial status unverified.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: Mem0
providers.summary: Memory layer for AI agents (hosted platform + open-source), with REST API, llms.txt, and the official OpenMemory MCP server.
providers.scope: Hosted platform + open-source

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: Open-source mem0 runs locally as a full test environment.

automation_permitted.notes: ToS not yet reviewed and recorded.

providers.name: MiniMax

providers.name: Telegram Bot API

scoped_tokens.notes: Bot tokens are all-or-nothing for the bot's own API; per-chat permissions are set by admins. Needs a docs framing as evidence.

token_revocation.notes: BotFather's /revoke regenerates the token, invalidating the old one.

sandbox_or_test_mode.notes: A dedicated test environment with separate servers and bots is documented.

free_tier_or_trial.notes: The Bot API is free.

automation_permitted.notes: Bots are the product; automation through the Bot API is the intended use.
